title: Financial support provided through the Green Climate Fund
subtitle: The total financial support provided to developing countries for climate change mitigation and adaptation. Countries have set a collective goal of mobilizing $100 billion per year from 2020 onwards.
caption:
Financial support provided through the Green Climate Fund

The total financial support provided to developing countries for climate change mitigation and adaptation. Countries have set a collective goal of mobilizing $100 billion per year from 2020 onwards.

The line chart tracks the total financial support provided through the Green Climate Fund from 2011 to 2022, measured in current US dollars per year. The data series begins in 2011 at a value just under $30 billion and remains flat at that level through 2012. A sharp increase occurs between 2012 and 2013, where the value rises to approximately $41 billion. From 2013 to 2016, the trend shows a steady upward trajectory, reaching a local peak of roughly $52 billion in 2016. Following this, the line dips slightly in 2017 to just under $50 billion before recovering to a higher point of approximately $56 billion in 2018. The values for 2019 and 2020 show a minor decline, settling around $53 billion. Starting in 2020, the chart displays a steep acceleration; the line climbs rapidly past $60 billion in 2021 and continues to rise sharply, ending at the series maximum of $80 billion in 2022. This final data point represents the highest level of support recorded in the displayed period, significantly exceeding the values observed in the preceding decade. The subtitle notes a collective goal of mobilizing $100 billion per year from 2020 onwards, a threshold that the 2022 value approaches but does not yet reach within the timeframe shown.
